questions.question: How can you create a new page on the desktop or web version of Notion?

options.text: Click the 📝 icon located at the top of your left sidebar.
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that clicking the 📝 icon at the top of your left sidebar is a primary way to add a new page on desktop and web.

options.text: Press the Cmd/Ctrl + N shortcut on your keyboard.
options.explanation: The Cmd/Ctrl + N shortcut is specifically mentioned for the desktop app, not the web version.

options.text: Tap the + button at the top of the screen.
options.explanation: The + button is not mentioned in the article; the 📝 icon is used for desktop/web, while mobile uses a different tap location.

options.text: Type the word 'new' in the search bar.
options.explanation: The article does not mention using the search bar to create pages; it focuses on the sidebar icon and shortcuts.

questions.question: What is the keyboard shortcut to create a new page in the desktop app?

options.text: Cmd/Ctrl + P
options.explanation: Cmd/Ctrl + P is typically used for printing, not creating a new page.

options.text: Cmd/Ctrl + N
options.explanation: The article specifies that using the shortcut cmd/ctrl + N creates a new page in the desktop app.

options.text: Cmd/Ctrl + T
options.explanation: Cmd/Ctrl + T is not a standard Notion shortcut for creating pages.

options.text: Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + N
options.explanation: This shortcut is not mentioned in the article; the standard shortcut is Cmd/Ctrl + N.

questions.question: On a mobile device, where is the button to create a new page located?

options.text: At the top of the left sidebar.
options.explanation: Mobile interfaces differ from desktop; the sidebar is not the primary location for creating pages on mobile.

options.text: At the bottom of the screen.
options.explanation: The article notes that on mobile, you tap the 📝 icon located at the bottom of the screen.

options.text: In the settings menu.
options.explanation: New pages are created directly in the workspace, not within the settings menu.

options.text: In the center of the screen.
options.explanation: The specific location mentioned is the bottom of the screen.

questions.question: What happens when you select an option at the bottom of the page on desktop or web?

options.text: The page is immediately deleted.
options.explanation: Selecting an option formats the page, it does not delete it.

options.text: The page is formatted accordingly.
options.explanation: The article states that selecting an option formats the page to match that choice (e.g., import, template, table).

options.text: It opens the YouTube video embedded on the page.
options.explanation: The video is an advertisement or placeholder; clicking page options does not affect the video.

options.text: It saves the file to your computer's hard drive.
options.explanation: Notion saves content automatically in the cloud; selecting options does not trigger a local save.

questions.question: What functionality is unlocked when you highlight text on a page?

options.text: You can change the color or style of the text.
options.explanation: Highlighting text brings up an edit bar where you can change color, style, add comments, or hyperlinks.

options.text: You can delete the entire page.
options.explanation: Deleting the page requires a specific menu option, not just highlighting text.

options.text: You can create a new database.
options.explanation: Creating a database is typically done via slash commands, not by highlighting existing text.

options.text: You can export the page to PDF.
options.explanation: Exporting is usually a separate action from editing text formatting.

questions.question: What is the purpose of typing the `/` key in Notion?

options.text: It brings up a menu of block types.
options.explanation: The article explains that typing `/` brings up a menu of all available block types (like headings, checkboxes, etc.).

options.text: It opens the help center documentation.
options.explanation: The help center link is separate; the slash command is for content creation.

options.text: It saves the current page to your computer.
options.explanation: Saving is automatic; `/` is used for inserting specific content blocks.

options.text: It creates a new database table.
options.explanation: While databases can be created via slash commands, the `/` itself opens the menu of options, not just the table.

questions.question: Which specific slash command creates a large heading?

options.text: /header
options.explanation: While headers exist, the article specifically uses `/h1` as the example for a large heading.
The article explicitly states that typing `/h1` creates a large heading.

options.text: /title
options.explanation: The article does not list `/title` as a valid block command example.

options.text: /head
options.explanation: The article uses `/h1` as the correct example for a large heading.

questions.question: When you type `/`, where are your most used and recently used blocks located?

options.text: At the bottom of the menu.
options.explanation: The article states these frequently used blocks are at the top of the menu for quick access.

options.text: At the top of the menu.
options.explanation: The article notes that a combination of your most used and recently used blocks appear at the top of the menu.

options.text: Inside the left sidebar.
options.explanation: The sidebar contains pages, while block types appear when typing `/`.

options.text: They are not displayed; you must search for them.
options.explanation: Notion displays these blocks at the top of the menu for convenience.

questions.question: What is the first step to take once your new page opens?

options.text: Give your page a title.
options.explanation: The article states that you can give your page a title and start jotting down your thoughts as the first step.

options.text: Select a template.
options.explanation: Templates are an option to get started, but the text says you 'can give your page a title' first.

options.text: Import a file.
options.explanation: Importing is one of the options at the bottom, but not the initial first step mentioned.

options.text: Type a slash command.
options.explanation: While you can type slash commands, you typically need a title first to organize your thoughts.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as an option available at the bottom of the page on desktop or web?

options.text: Import from an app or file.
options.explanation: The article explicitly lists importing as an option to get started.

options.text: Use a template.
options.explanation: Using a template is listed as an option to get started.

options.text: Create a table.
options.explanation: Creating a table is listed as an option to get started.

options.text: Change the system theme.
options.explanation: Changing the system theme is a system-wide setting, not an option for starting a new page.
